IXACTSoundBank::Prepare

Primes a sound for playback with zero latency.

HRESULT Prepare(
    DWORD dwSoundCueIndex,
    DWORD dwFlags,
    PXACTSOUNDCUE *ppSoundCue
);

Parameters

dwSoundCueIndex
[in] A value indicating the index of the sound cue to be primed.
dwFlags
[in] Flags affecting preparation. May be the following:
Value Description
XACT_FLAG_SOUNDCUE_PRIME Indicates that the sound cue should be primed.

This flag is optional.
XACT_FLAG_SOUNDCUE_USE_LAST_VARIATION Indicates that the sound cue should use the last sound selected, assuming that multiple sounds are associated with the cue. That sound will continue to be selected until the function is called without specifying XACT_FLAG_SOUNDCUE_USE_LAST_VARIATION, at which point the sounds are selected at random again.

This does not affect wave variations. If the sound has a play event with wave variations, the waves will be selected at random. Similarly, pitch and volume variations are unaffected by this flag.

If no previous sound has been selected before using this flag, a sound will be selected at random.

This flag is optional.
ppSoundCue
[out] The address of an IXACTSoundCue pointer to be returned. This parameter is passed to the IXACTSoundBank::Play command and cannot be NULL.

Return Values

Returns DS_OK if successful; returns an error code otherwise.

Remarks

Prepare can be used for priming (reading ahead) streaming data, or can be used any time the game needs control of the sound cue instance before playback actually starts.

Priming a sound cue with this function enables the sound to play with zero latency. This can be particularly helpful when synchronization between speech and animations are required. When the sound is primed, a small portion of the sound data is read into memory and immediate playback can begin (thus avoiding the latency involved with reading the data directly from DVD or hard disk). The Prepare function is intended to be used with wave bank entries that have not been marked as Enable zero-latency Streaming in the wave bank. Waves marked as Enable zero-latency Streaming are primed automatically when the wave bank is registered, and StreamDataAvailable notification is sent (so calling Prepare is unnecessary).

Note    Enable zero-latency Streaming is a property of an entry in a wave bank, rather than the wave bank itself.

Calling the Prepare function will return immediately. To determine if the cue is ready (has been primed), register for a cue notification using type eXACTNotification_StreamDataAvailable.

To determine if all the wave bank entries in a given wave bank have been primed, register for a wave bank notification using type eXACTNotification_StreamDataAvailable. When this notification has been received, IXACTSoundBank::Play can be called on any cue that uses the wave bank entries without latency.

Note    When registering for notification type eXACTNotification_StreamDataAvailable on a streamed wave bank with no zero-latency wave entries, the notification will fire immediately.

Cues that are already playing cannot be primed.

To unprepare a sound cue (for example, a sound cue was prepared for an animation that never took place), Stop can be called on the sound cue instance. Even though the sound never started playing, this will successfully stop and free the primed sound.

The layer of a sound cue is not applied until the cue is played. Calling IXACTSoundBank::Play with the XACT_FLAG_SOUNDCUE_PRIME or calling Prepare will not interrupt playing cues.

For more information on priming, see the XACT Concepts section. Also, see the XACTParamControl sample for an example of using priming.

Requirements

Header: Declared in XACT.h.

Import Library: Use XACTEng.lib.

See Also

IXACTEngine::RegisterNotification, IXACTEngine::GetNotification
